meaning: n.眨眼,使眼色,瞬间,(星光等的)闪烁vi.眨眼,使眼色,闪烁,打信号,假装不见,终止,熄灭vt.眨(眼)
examples: He gave her a knowing wink.
'I know you understand,' he said and gave a conspiratorial wink.
The satellite disappeared in a wink.
Wink's cofounder, Brian Dougherty, adapted the technology he had created for his prior company, Geoworks.
Wink's service is free to the consumer and doesn't require another set-top box, as does WebTV.
